questions.prompt_html: Which of the graphs below are functions of x?
questions.prompt_text: Which of the graphs below are functions of x? Short Description: A graph (a) shows an upward opening parabola with vertex in the third quadrant. The parabola slopes upward to the second quadrant on the left and slopes upward in the first through the fourth quadrant on the right. Short Description: A graph (b)shows a curve that is concave down left to the origin and concave up right to the origin. Long Description: The curve slopes upward from the third quadrant, goes to maximum value in the second quadrant, and then slopes downward to the fourth quadrant passing through the origin. The curve goes to a minimum value in the fourth quadrant and then turns upward to move to the first quadrant. Short Description: A graph (c) shows a rightward opening parabola with vertex in second quadrant. The curve moves to the first quadrant at the top and moves to the fourth quadrant through the third quadrant at the bottom. Short Description: A graph (d) shows a concave up rectilinear graph consisting of three straight lines. Long Description: A line parallel to x axis lies in the third and fourth quadrants and meets other two straight lines at its end points. At one end point, the line parallel to x axis meets a line of positive slope that lies in first and fourth quadrants. At another end point, the line parallel to x axis meets another line of negative slope that lies in second and third quadrants.

choices.label: E
choices.html: None of the above graphs are functions of x.
choices.text: None of the above graphs are functions of x .

images.alt: Short Description: A graph (a) shows an upward opening parabola with vertex in the third quadrant. The parabola slopes upward to the second quadrant on the left and slopes upward in the first through the fourth quadrant on the right.

Short Description: A graph (b)shows a curve that is concave down left to the origin and concave up right to the origin. Long Description: The curve slopes upward from the third quadrant, goes to maximum value in the second quadrant, and then slopes downward to the fourth quadrant passing through the origin. The curve goes to a minimum value in the fourth quadrant and then turns upward to move to the first quadrant.

Short Description: A graph (c) shows a rightward opening parabola with vertex in second quadrant. The curve moves to the first quadrant at the top and moves to the fourth quadrant through the third quadrant at the bottom.

Short Description: A graph (d) shows a concave up rectilinear graph consisting of three straight lines. Long Description: A line parallel to x axis lies in the third and fourth quadrants and meets other two straight lines at its end points. At one end point, the line parallel to x axis meets a line of positive slope that lies in first and fourth quadrants. At another end point, the line parallel to x axis meets another line of negative slope that lies in second and third quadrants.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The distance an object is from the ground after being tossed from a hot air balloon 770 feet in the air is a function of time: f(t) where f(t) is height and t is the amount of time the object has been in the air. Predict the height of the object after 5.5 seconds.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The distance an object is from the ground after being tossed from a hot air balloon 770 feet in the air is a function of time: f(t) = -16.1(t) with superscript (2) + 5.5t + 770, where f(t) is height and t is the amount of time the object has been in the air. Predict the height of the object after 5.5 seconds.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A marine biologist determines that the size, p, of a population of crabs, after t days can be modeled by the function   Assuming that this model continues to be accurate, when will this population become extinct? (Round to the nearest day.)
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. A marine biologist determines that the size, p, of a population of crabs, after t days can be modeled by the function p(t) = -0.00003(t) with superscript (3) + 0.008(t) with superscript (2) + 3.5t + 600. Assuming that this model continues to be accurate, when will this population become extinct? (Round to the nearest day.)

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure.

a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3.
b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and 
c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after  Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure. a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3. b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and (8, 31). c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after t = 6. Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
a. 9.7, the wind is increasing in speed at an average rate of 9.7 mph per hour
b. -3.4, the wind speed is decreasing in speed at an average rate of 3.4 mph per hour 
c. The storm reached its closest point about 6:00 a.m. and is moving away after that.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

When an object falling from rest encounters air resistance proportional to the square of its velocity, the distance it falls (in meters) after t seconds is given by where m is the mass of the object in kilograms, g = 9.8 m/s2 is the acceleration due to gravity, and k is a physical constant. A BASE jumper  leaps from a tall cliff and performs a ten-second delay (she free falls for 10 s and then opens her chute). How far does she fall in 10 s? Assume  Round the answer to one decimal place if necessary.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. When an object falling from rest encounters air resistance proportional to the square of its velocity, the distance it falls (in meters) after t seconds is given by d(t) = (m/k) ln[cosh(square root of ((kg/m)) t)], where m is the mass of the object in kilograms, g = 9.8 m/s 2 is the acceleration due to gravity, and k is a physical constant. A BASE jumper (m = 76 kg) leaps from a tall cliff and performs a ten-second delay (she free falls for 10 s and then opens her chute). How far does she fall in 10 s? Assume k = 0.2. Round the answer to one decimal place if necessary.

questions.prompt_html: Identify and sketch the curve represented by the equation  -  = 1.
questions.prompt_text: Identify and sketch the curve represented by the equation ((x) with superscript (2)/4) - ((y) with superscript (2)/9) = 1.
questions.solution_html:
A hyperbola with centre at the origin and vertices at (±2, 0). 
Its asymptotes are y = ±  x.
questions.solution_text: A hyperbola with centre at the origin and vertices at (±2, 0). Its asymptotes are y = ± (3/2) x . Short Description: A graph of a hyperbola whose center lies at origin and vertices lies at (negative 2, 0) and (2, 0). Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 8 to 8 in increments of 1 and the y axis ranges from negative 8 to 8 in increments of 1. The branch on the passes through points (negative 2, 0), (negative 4, 5.2), and (negative 4, negative 5.2). The branch on the right passes through points (2, 0), (4, 5.2), and (4, negative 5.2). Two dashed lines for y = negative three halves x and y = three halves x are the asymptotes of the hyperbola. Both the lines pass through the origin. The values used in the description are approximate.

Its asymptotes are y = ±  x.

questions.prompt_text: Identify and sketch the graph of the equation 16x 2 - 9y 2 - 128x - 18y + 103 = 0.
questions.solution_html: Completing the squares we arrive at  -  = 1. This is a hyperbola with centre at (4,-1), transverse axis from (1, -1) to (7, -1), and asymptotes y + 1 = ±  (x - 4).
questions.solution_text: Completing the squares we arrive at (((x - 4)) with superscript (2)/9) - (((y + 1)) with superscript (2)/16) = 1. This is a hyperbola with centre at (4,-1), transverse axis from (1, -1) to (7, -1), and asymptotes y + 1 = ± (4/3) (x - 4). Short Description: A graph of a hyperbola whose center lies at (4, negative 1), and vertices lies at (1, negative 1) and (7, negative 1). Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 4 to 12 in increments of 1 and the y axis ranges from negative 10 to 8 in increments of 1. The branch on the left passes through points (1, negative 1), (0, 2.53), and (0, negative 2.53). The branch on the right passes through points (7, negative 1), (10, 5.93), and (10, negative 5.93). Two dashed lines for y = negative 1 + four thirds of left parenthesis x minus 4 right parenthesis and y = negative 1 minus four thirds of right parenthesis left parenthesis x minus 4 right parenthesis are the asymptotes of the hyperbola. Both the lines pass through the point (4, negative 1). The values used in the description are approximate.

feedback_html.solution: Completing the squares we arrive at  -  = 1. This is a hyperbola with centre at (4,-1), transverse axis from (1, -1) to (7, -1), and asymptotes y + 1 = ±  (x - 4).

Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 4 to 12 in increments of 1 and the y axis ranges from negative 10 to 8 in increments of 1. The branch on the left passes through points (1, negative 1), (0, 2.53), and (0, negative 2.53). The branch on the right passes through points (7, negative 1), (10, 5.93), and (10, negative 5.93). Two dashed lines for y = negative 1 + four thirds of left parenthesis x minus 4 right parenthesis and y = negative 1 minus four thirds of right parenthesis left parenthesis x minus 4 right parenthesis are the asymptotes of the hyperbola. The upper end of the curve approaches negative x axis and the lower end approaches a dashed vertical line at x = 1.

questions.solution_text: Short Description: A graph shows two curves and a straight line. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 4 to 4 in increments of 0.5 and the y axis ranges from negative 3 to 5.5 in increments of 0.5. The first curve is a concave up curve in the second quadrant from an open dot at (negative 1, 1) to (negative 2, 0). The curve then slopes upward in the second quadrant. A straight line slopes upward from an open dot at (negative 1, negative 1) to an open dot at (1, 1) passing through the origin. The second curve is a concave down curve in the first quadrant sloping upward from the point (1, 0).

The first curve is a concave up curve in the second quadrant from an open dot at (negative 1, 1) to (negative 2, 0). The curve then slopes upward in the second quadrant. A straight line slopes upward from an open dot at (negative 1, negative 1) to an open dot at (1, 1) passing through the origin. The second curve is a concave down curve in the first quadrant sloping upward from the point (1, 0).
